name: A Coruña
summary: A Coruña is a sparkly seaside city on a peninsula in green Galicia, northwest Spain, where Atlantic waves crash against rocky cliffs. Its biggest superstar is the Tower of Hercules, a Roman lighthouse that has been guiding ships for almost 2,000 years and still works today! From here, pilgrims can also follow an ancient walking path called the Camino to the famous cathedral in Santiago de Compostela.
funFacts: The Tower of Hercules is the world's oldest working lighthouse. Romans built it over 1,900 years ago and its light still warns ships at sea every night.
People in Galicia speak their own language called Galego, which sounds a bit like a mix of Spanish and Portuguese. 'Hello' is 'Ola'.
A Coruña is nicknamed the Crystal City because hundreds of houses have white glass balconies that sparkle in the sun and shelter people from the rainy Atlantic weather.
An old legend says the hero Hercules battled a giant named Geryon for three days here, then buried the giant's head and built a city on top.
The famous local dish is pulpo (octopus), boiled, snipped with scissors onto a wooden plate, sprinkled with paprika and sea salt, and eaten with toothpicks!
thingsToDo: Climb the spiral staircase inside the Tower of Hercules, the world's oldest working Roman lighthouse, for huge views over the Atlantic Ocean and rocky coast.
Meet sharks, octopuses and a friendly grey seal named Nemo at the Aquarium Finisterrae, which has touch pools and an underwater Nautilus room.
Explore the human body with hands-on experiments at the Domus museum (also called Casa do Home), a curving science building right above the sea.
Watch a planetarium show and balance the giant Foucault pendulum at the Casa de las Ciencias science museum inside Santa Margarita park.
Wander Praza de María Pita, the huge arcaded main square named after a local heroine, then walk along the glass-galleried Marina to Riazor beach for sandcastles.
Take a train or coach about an hour east to Santiago de Compostela to see the giant cathedral where Camino pilgrims arrive after walking hundreds of kilometres.

images.alt: The ancient Tower of Hercules lighthouse glows at sunrise on a rocky headland above the Atlantic in A Coruña.
A long row of white glass-fronted balconies sparkles along A Coruña's Marina seafront, the city's famous galerías.
Plaza de María Pita's grand arcaded square sits in front of A Coruña's elegant city hall with its blue domes.
Riazor's wide golden sand beach curves along the city, with gentle Atlantic waves rolling in.
The towering stone west façade of Santiago de Compostela Cathedral rises above the Obradoiro square.
